summary: Ukraine's parliament approved a record defence budget increase, while Kyiv seeks licences to produce Patriot PAC‑3 missiles. Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV attacks across several oblasts and struck the Tuapse refinery and Russian air‑defence assets with Storm Shadow missiles. Russian air‑defence forces repelled a mass attack in Volgograd region and Hungary reaffirmed it will not supply weapons to Ukraine.
key_points: Ukraine's parliament approved a defence budget increase to 1.56 trillion hryvnias, funded partly by EU assistance (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).
President Zelensky urged the US to grant licences for Ukrainian production of Patriot PAC‑3 missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81517).
Ukrainian Storm Shadow missiles hit the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fire and smoke (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Russian air‑defence forces intercepted a mass UAV attack in Volgograd oblast, with no casualties reported (Source: @tass_agency/377992).
Hungary notified NATO it will continue not to supply weapons or military equipment to Ukraine (Source: @tass_agency/377982).

Multiple UAV groups were reported over Ukrainian regions including Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kharkiv, targeting cities such as Reni and Renі (Source: @eRadarrua/94089, @kpszsu/63146, @kpszsu/63144, @kpszsu/63136).
British intelligence estimated Russian military deaths at nearly half a million since the invasion began (Source: @UkraineNow/81502).

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine's parliament approved a record defence budget increase, and Kyiv is pursuing licences to produce Patriot PAC‑3 missiles. Ukrainian forces have intensified UAV attacks across multiple oblasts and struck the Tuapse refinery with Storm Shadow missiles. Russian air‑defence repelled a mass UAV attack in Volgograd and Hungary reiterated its refusal to supply weapons to Ukraine.

points: The Verkhovna Rada approved a defence budget of 1.56 trillion hryvnias, with funds coming from EU external financial assistance and earmarked military taxes for soldier pay, weapons procurement and equipment renewal (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).

President Zelensky sent an urgent letter to US President Trump and Congress requesting licences for domestic production of Patriot PAC‑3 missiles, citing a critical shortage of air‑defence systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81517).

Ukrainian forces used Storm Shadow missiles to strike the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fire, smoke and unspecified damage to a plant processing about 12 million tonnes of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Ukrainian air units reportedly targeted Russian air‑defence command and control equipment in Voronezh region with Storm Shadow missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).

Multiple UAV groups were observed over Ukrainian territories: two groups over Dnipropetrovsk heading to Pokrovsk, several over Odesa heading south, and groups from the Black Sea heading toward Reni and Orlivka (Source: @eRadarrua/94089, @kpszsu/63144, @kpszsu/63146).
Ukrainian officials announced plans to purchase up to 20 JAS‑39 Gripen E‑modification fighters from Sweden, plus 16 older variants as aid (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30505).
Zelensky met with US Democratic congressmen to discuss urgent need for anti‑missile missiles due to constant Russian shelling (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30483).
Russian air‑defence forces in Volgograd oblast repelled a mass UAV attack; a residential building was hit but no casualties were reported (Source: @tass_agency/377992).
Hungary announced to NATO it will continue not to supply weapons or military equipment to Ukraine, maintaining the policy of the previous government (Source: @tass_agency/377982).
President Putin stated Russia understands both capabilities and risks of AI implementation (Source: @tass_agency/377962).
Russian mobile fire groups of the Leningrad Air Force and Air Defence demonstrated UAV interception on a training range in Leningrad oblast (Source: @tass_agency/377995).
Russian forces in the Volgograd region reported no casualties after the UAV attack on a multi‑storey residential building (Source: @tass_agency/377992).
Russian authorities registered the first domestic set of reagents for detecting avian influenza A(H5N8) (Source: @tass_agency/377991).
Russian Foreign Ministry head Lavrov called EU demands for Russian troop limitations 'idiotic' (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30495).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea were reported heading toward the southern Odesa direction, targeting locations such as Reni and Orlivka (Source: @kpszsu/63144, @mon1tor_ua/67256).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over Kharkiv region originating from Belgorod oblast, Russia (Source: @kpszsu/63146).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAVs over Sumy heading toward Lebedyn and over Dnipropetrovsk toward Prosyana/Chaplyne (Source: @eRadarrua/94083, @kpszsu/63136).
Ukrainian officials noted deteriorating situation in Kostiantynivka with Russian units entrenching in surrounding villages and controlling the skies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30485).
Ukrainian air‑defence reportedly engaged Russian UAVs over the Leningrad region during training exercises (Source: @tass_agency/377995).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have struck Russian air‑defence automation systems in Voronezh region (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Ukrainian reports of UAV attacks over Chernihiv heading toward Mena (Source: @kpszsu/63137).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region heading toward Mena (Source: @kpszsu/63137).
Ukrainian reports of UAV activity along the Dnipro corridor near Dobryanka in Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/94106).
Ukrainian sources noted a new reactive UAV over Sumy near Buryn (Source: @eRadarrua/94083).
Ukrainian forces reported UAVs over the northern Chernihiv direction heading toward Mena (Source: @kpszsu/63137).
No direct combat engagements were detailed for the northern front in the provided messages.
No additional specific unit or casualty data were provided for the northern sector.
No further operational details were available.
Ukrainian Storm Shadow missiles struck the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fire and smoke (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Ukrainian UAV groups from the Black Sea were reported heading south over Odesa toward Reni and Orlivka (Source: @kpszsu/63144, @mon1tor_ua/67256).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV attacks over the southern Odesa region, including a group heading toward Reni (Source: @kpszsu/63144).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV activity over the southern Odesa direction, with about ten Shahed drones over the Black Sea near Izmail district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67256).
Ukrainian reports of UAV attacks over the southern Odesa region originating from the Black Sea (Source: @kpszsu/63144).
No specific ground engagements were detailed for the southern front beyond the Tuapse strike.
No casualty figures were provided for southern operations.
Ukrainian Storm Shadow missiles were used to hit Russian air‑defence automation systems in Voronezh and the Tuapse refinery (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Russian air‑defence forces repelled a mass UAV attack in Volgograd oblast, with a residential building hit but no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/377992).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported across multiple oblasts, including Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/94089, @kpszsu/63146, @kpszsu/63144).
Hungary announced it will not supply weapons or military equipment to Ukraine, affecting Ukraine's air‑defence procurement (Source: @tass_agency/377982).
Russian mobile fire groups demonstrated UAV interception capabilities in Leningrad oblast (Source: @tass_agency/377995).
Ukrainian officials emphasized a critical need for anti‑missile missiles due to constant Russian shelling (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30483).
No direct air combat incidents involving fighter aircraft were detailed in the messages.

key_developments: Ukraine's parliament approved a record defence budget of 1.56 trillion hryvnias, with EU assistance earmarked for soldier pay, weapons procurement and equipment renewal (Source: @UkraineNow/81510).
President Zelensky requested US licences for Ukrainian production of Patriot PAC‑3 missiles, highlighting a critical shortage of air‑defence systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81517).

Ukrainian Storm Shadow missiles struck the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar region, causing fire, smoke and potential disruption to fuel supplies for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81505).
Russian air‑defence forces repelled a mass UAV attack in Volgograd oblast; a residential building was hit but no casualties were reported (Source: @tass_agency/377992).
Hungary reaffirmed to NATO it will continue not to supply weapons or military equipment to Ukraine, maintaining the policy of the previous government (Source: @tass_agency/377982).

Multiple Ukrainian UAV groups were reported over Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, Chernihiv, Sumy and Kharkiv regions, targeting cities such as Reni, Orlivka and Lebedyn (Source: @eRadarrua/94089, @kpszsu/63146, @kpszsu/63144).
British intelligence estimated Russian military deaths at nearly half a million since the invasion began, marking the first such public estimate by a senior UK official (Source: @UkraineNow/81502).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air‑defence remains capable of intercepting mass UAV attacks, as demonstrated in Volgograd, but Ukrainian UAV proliferation across multiple fronts indicates a growing low‑altitude threat to Russian rear areas. The refusal of Hungary to supply weapons limits Ukraine's ability to replenish air‑defence assets, potentially affecting its capacity to counter Russian air operations. Ukraine's pursuit of domestic Patriot missile production and acquisition of Gripen fighters suggests an effort to close the air‑defence gap, though reliance on foreign licences remains a vulnerability.
sections.regional_response: The European Union is providing financial assistance for Ukraine's defence budget increase, while NATO members such as Hungary maintain a non‑support stance on arms deliveries. Ukraine's diplomatic outreach to the United States seeks to secure critical air‑defence licences and missile supplies. Regional actors like Romania are implicated in UAV attacks, highlighting the spill‑over risk to neighboring NATO states.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian UAV activity is widespread, with groups operating from the Black Sea and Russian territories, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure in southern and eastern Ukraine. Russian statements on AI capabilities and domestic pandemic preparedness indicate a focus on technological advancement alongside conventional operations. British casualty estimates, though unverified, suggest significant Russian personnel losses, potentially impacting morale and force readiness.
